name: imbalanced-learn (Class Imbalance Sampling)

performance.notes: Sampling performance depends on dataset size and method

agent_readiness.notes: scikit-learn compatible sampling for imbalanced datasets. Over-sampling: SMOTE, ADASYN, RandomOverSampler. Under-sampling: RandomUnderSampler, NearMiss, TomekLinks, ClusterCentroids. Combination: SMOTEENN, SMOTETomek. sampler.fit_resample(X, y). Pipeline compatible with sklearn. make_pipeline(SMOTE(), LogisticRegression()). Essential for fraud detection, medical diagnosis, anomaly detection where class imbalance is severe (1:100, 1:1000 ratios). Part of scikit-learn-contrib.

gotchas: SMOTE only works on numerical features
Over-sampling should only be applied to training set (not test)
Synthetic samples may not capture real-world distribution well
Some methods are slow for very large datasets
best_when: Classification problems with severe class imbalance (fraud, medical, anomaly)
Any sklearn pipeline needing resampling before fitting
Research needing multiple resampling strategies to compare
Production ML pipelines with class imbalance
avoid_when: Non-tabular data (deep learning handles imbalance differently)
summary: imbalanced-learn provides resampling strategies for imbalanced classification problems. SMOTE, ADASYN (over-sampling), RandomUnderSampler, NearMiss (under-sampling), combined SMOTEENN. fit_resample(X, y). sklearn Pipeline compatible. Essential for fraud detection, medical diagnosis, and anomaly detection with 1:100+ class ratios. Apply only to training data.
